reviews.review_description:
It’s an entertaining game, and certainly one I would enjoy if I just wanna be lazy on the couch.

However I would consider some of the bugs to make it a little bit of a mess, mostly the collision properties of the elements on the sides of the roads. Catching a branch or a post sticking out slightly can launch you off the other side of the road.

Also maybe adding details like Weight and Special Abilities would be useful. The Viper is featherweight and my first run was a Hot Run on the Moon level. It would literally spin out and actually crash with nearly every collision. That didn’t exactly make for a fun time.

It’s an experience, I can’t exactly call it either good nor bad, but it certainly in need of some polish before it can be considered a good one. There just isn’t enough there to call it a bad game, either.

Edit: Knocked one star for having memory leak crashes and more rigged-feeling missions. (I.e. Over take X cars in the air with the Viper using only 3 jumps; Car spawns were sparse. and Collect coins in less than 4 seconds apart; but they’re spawing in 5 seconds away.)

Please keep working on this game.

This is like one of those endless road, mobile phone games, but it isn’t. Each track in the game has an end. I’m not sure if you’re supposed to avoid other cars or hit them since aside from slowing your car, there doesn’t seem to be any penalty. I have never failed to complete a race, never run out of time or lost due to crashing. But for some reason I can’t stop playing it, but it remains to be seen if I’ll be quite so keen once I’ve unlocked all the cars.

I hope this game is still in development and hasn’t been abandoned.

The game exited to desktop with a low memory error on a couple of occasions.

This game brings 80s vibes similar to the game out run. It's fun to play but isnt quite there yet but is in a good way seeing though it is still a work in progress. Keep up the good work developers and this will be a gem of a game

reviews.review_title: Should be in main quest store
reviews.review_description: Out run comes to the quest. Fantastic graphics and gameplay.  Really addictive.  Looking foreword to any updates.  The Devs should be really proud of this. Why is it not in the main store it’s a travesty

The game is amazing cars look great story so far is good and I enjoy the replayability of trying to get new cars the maps are fun. But you can't grab your steering wheel with your hands and drive and that's one of the best parts of VR and only 7 maps so far need more maps and keep adding cars other then that great game I love it keep up the good work.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback. At the moment what we are sure to add are many more levels in the coming weeks. The support for driving by moving the controller has been added to our other driving games (Touring Karts and Echo Wars) but in an inRun with so much jumping we don't see that it is very reliable because without realizing it you are putting your hand away from the steering wheel. In any case, we will be adding several improvements and listening to your feedback.

reviews.review_description: This isn't bad at all, but it's a little glitchy in that the gameplay will stop for a split second every now and again. My main complaint though is the robotic voiceover. I found it very annoying and would have much preferred an over-the-top, over-enthusiastic arcade style voiceover to match the gameplay.

I can't say I would buy this game at full price, but it's amusing enough for what I did pay for it. My main issue is the car's control though as well as the often times blinding special effects, ridiculous lighting, and the massive amount of reaffic mixed with the floaty controls and csmera ALL making proper maneuvering without crashing (or uncontrollably going off thevroad) nearly impossible.
I couldn't even go off to a portion of the off road section (it had arrows and everything) because my car REFUSED to keep going right, which ended in me crashing into a big rock RIGHT NEXT TO THE OFF-ROAD TRAIL I WAS TRYING TO GET ON!
Luckily there's little, if any, actual punishment for crashing, falling off the road, or any other blunders you'll inevitably keep dealing with due to very poor controls & camera (and sometimes inability to even SEE the road). But it does make many "missions" MUCH harder, if not impossible, to 100% complete no matter how well you are otherwise doing...

So can't recommend this game if you are paying more than $5 for it currently..
The driving mechanics are pretty fun and there are many tracks and cars. Otherwise it kind of seems like a mobile game.

This is a solid Outrun type game… not perfect yet, but definitely good enough to purchase. I came here expecting to see AT LEAST 60-70 reviews but wouldn’t have been surprised to see 150. But 8 reviews!? No way, this game deserves way more respect than that imo. 
At first I wasn’t a fan of the controls all that much but I realized I gotta upgrade to get better controlling cars. There is a stutter however that occurs during every scene change that needs to be fixed ASAP. It can cause you to crash, or just give you straight up headaches.  Other than that, I can’t find much fault in this game.
Played it for like 20 mins and it's just not fun. Should've been made as a phone game not a vr game

Why can’t I connect? Everything else is perfect. 

Now it’ll cut off on me saying it’s not enough memory but my oculus has plenty of memory. Sorry before ready player me it would cut off in the middle of a race. Still perfection

developer_response.body: If you restart the Quest, the problem is solved. It seems that ReadyPlayerMe uses a lot of RAM, and depending on the state of the Quest, it costs more than necessary when running the ReadyPlayerMe editor.

reviews.review_description:
Just purchased this around an hour ago. So far so good. Graphics are Crisp and appealing. Good amount of cars to choose from and customize once you get enough cards to unlock them. A few different camera angles to choose the view that best fits you. Each course presents goals to achieve that make things more entertaining. All in all I'm having a good time so far. 

My only complaint is there are these brief moments during a race where it seems to lag for a second or 2. Whenever there is a big change in scenery there tends to be a little pause. Besides that little hiccup everything else is looking good.
